title: Chennai Om Sri Skandhashramam
tagline: Chennai Om Sri Skandhashramam — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: Chennai Om Sri Skandhashramam, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Chennai Om Sri Skandashramam is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Murugan (Swaminatha Swami) in Chennai, India.
overview.description: Chennai Om Sri Skandashramam is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Murugan (Swaminatha Swami) in Chennai, India. It is located at Selaiyur, Tambaram, a southern neighbourhood of Chennai. The temple is known of its huge idols of several deities, including Kamala Siddhi Vinayakar, Panchamukha Heramba Ganapathy, Dattatreya, Panchamukha Hanuman, Shaniswara, Ayyapan, goddess Ashtadashabhuja Durga Parameswari, Sarabeswara (form of Shiva), goddess Prathiyankira, goddess Bhuvaneshwari, Swaminathaswamy (chief deity at the temple), Sudarshanachakathalwar, Lakshmi Narasimhar, Maha Sahasralingamurthy (

topPlaces.name: Masjid-e-Anwari
topPlaces.description: The Masjid-e-Anwari is a mosque located on the outskirts of South Chennai, in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. The mosque is situated in the suburb of Rajakilpakkam, between Velachery and Tambaram, in the Tambaram Municipality in the Chengalpattu district.

topPlaces.name: Rajakilpakkam
topPlaces.description: Rajakilpakkam is a neighbourhood area of Chennai to the south of the city, officially a part of Tambaram Municipal Corporation from November 3, 2021, formerly a part of Sembakkam municipality, in Tamil Nadu, India. Its post code is 600073.

topPlaces.name: Selaiyur
topPlaces.description: Selaiyur is a suburb of Chennai, India, located on the Tambaram–Velachery Road in the Chennai Metropolitan Area. It was once called Silaiyur.

topPlaces.name: Sembakkam
topPlaces.description: Sembakkam is a neighborhood in the city of Tambaram, situated within the Chennai Metropolitan Area, Tamil Nadu, India. It falls under the Chennai Metropolitan Area (CMA) as notified in 1974.

topPlaces.name: Chitlapakkam Lake
topPlaces.description: Chitlapakkam Aeri, or Chitlapakkam Lake, is an urban lake located in Chitlapakkam, Chennai, India. It is the chief water body in the neighbourhood.

topPlaces.name: Chitlapakkam
topPlaces.description: Chitlapakkam is a residential locality located in Chennai Metropolitan Area governed by the Tambaram City Municipal Corporation. It is well known for its small, perennial lake and adjoining ecosystems.

topPlaces.name: Madambakkam Lake
topPlaces.description: Madambakkam Lake, or Madambakkam Aeri, is a rain-fed reservoir in Madambakkam, Chennai, India, lake serves water for agriculture field nearby and served a drinking water source. The lake covers an area of 250 acres.

topPlaces.name: Madambakkam
topPlaces.description: Madambakkam (Tamil: [maːɖambaːkkam]) is a neighborhood in the city of Tambaram, situated within the Chennai Metropolitan Area, Tamil Nadu, India. It borders Selaiyur in the west, Sembakkam in north and Vengaivasal in north east and Sithalapakkam in east.

items.note: The Masjid-e-Anwari is a mosque located on the outskirts of South Chennai, in the state of Tamil Nadu, India. The mosque is situated in the suburb of Rajakilpakkam, between Velachery and Tambaram, in the Tambaram Municipality in the Chengalpattu district.

Rajakilpakkam is a neighbourhood area of Chennai to the south of the city, officially a part of Tambaram Municipal Corporation from November 3, 2021, formerly a part of Sembakkam municipality, in Tamil Nadu, India. Its post code is 600073.
Selaiyur is a suburb of Chennai, India, located on the Tambaram–Velachery Road in the Chennai Metropolitan Area. It was once called Silaiyur.
Sembakkam is a neighborhood in the city of Tambaram, situated within the Chennai Metropolitan Area, Tamil Nadu, India. It falls under the Chennai Metropolitan Area (CMA) as notified in 1974.
Chitlapakkam Aeri, or Chitlapakkam Lake, is an urban lake located in Chitlapakkam, Chennai, India. It is the chief water body in the neighbourhood.

Chitlapakkam is a residential locality located in Chennai Metropolitan Area governed by the Tambaram City Municipal Corporation. It is well known for its small, perennial lake and adjoining ecosystems.

Madambakkam Lake, or Madambakkam Aeri, is a rain-fed reservoir in Madambakkam, Chennai, India, lake serves water for agriculture field nearby and served a drinking water source. The lake covers an area of 250 acres.

Madambakkam (Tamil: [maːɖambaːkkam]) is a neighborhood in the city of Tambaram, situated within the Chennai Metropolitan Area, Tamil Nadu, India. It borders Selaiyur in the west, Sembakkam in north and Vengaivasal in north east and Sithalapakkam in east.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chennai Om Sri Skandhashramam?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chennai Om Sri Skandhashramam is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chennai Om Sri Skandhashramam?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (9 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (23 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chennai Om Sri Skandhashramam?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chennai Om Sri Skandhashramam at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chennai Om Sri Skandhashramam?
faq.a: Stays in Chennai Om Sri Skandhashramam start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chennai Om Sri Skandhashramam?
faq.a: The top attractions are Masjid-e-Anwari, Rajakilpakkam, Selaiyur, Sembakkam, Chitlapakkam Lake.
